data.title: SGL Carbon - Outlook 2025 Adjusted EBITDA Between EUR 130 Mln And EUR 150 Mln
data.description:
SGL CARBON SE :

  • FY ADJUSTED EBITDA EUR 130-150 MILLION

  • DEVELOPING NEW SALES OPPORTUNITIES AND STRICT COST MANAGEMENT AS FOCUS FOR 2025

  • OUTLOOK 2025: CONSOLIDATED SALES FOR FISCAL YEAR 2025, INCLUDING ALL BUSINESS UNITS, TO BE SLIGHTLY BELOW PREVIOUS YEAR

  • OUTLOOK 2025: ADJUSTED EBITDA IN 2025 TO RANGE BETWEEN EUR 130 MILLION AND EUR 150 MILLION

  • OUTLOOK 2025: FCF EXCLUDING PAYMENTS FOR PLANNED RESTRUCTURING OF CF WILL BE BELOW PREVIOUS YEAR'S LEVEL BUT STILL POSITIVE

data.title: Sanofi to Acquire Autoimmune Disease Treatment From Dren Bio for Up to $1.9 Billion
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Sanofi said it would acquire Bispecific Myeloid Cell Engager, an autoimmune disease treatment, from Dren Bio, for up to $1.9 billion.

The French pharmaceutical company on Thursday said it would pay $600 million upfront and that the deal also includes potential future payments totaling $1.3 billion upon achievement of certain development and launch milestones. The transaction is expected to be financed with available cash resources, it said.

The deal seeks to broaden the company's immunology pipeline and is expected to close during the second quarter of 2025, it said.

"Deep B-cell depletion is at the frontier of treating autoimmune diseases and using the myeloid cell engager DR-0201 has the potential to elevate the treatment effect for patients, in particular patients refractory to existing treatments," Head of Research and Development Houman Ashrafian said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Hapag-Lloyd: Moderate Decline in Average Freight Rate is Anticipated
RocketBoots' Loss Prevention Trial Yields Positive ROI
data.description:
RocketBoots said its trial with a multinational retailer for its loss prevention software achieved the customer's objectives generating a positive return on investment, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company said it also met the customer's strict data security requirements with respect to the General Data Protection Regulation, a critical condition required as a part of securing a contract.

Whilst the trial value at the start of the trial was considered immaterial, it is now material due to ongoing extensions and variations with the customer having paid fees equal to approximately 8% of the company's 2024 revenue, the filing added.

The company's shares were up 7% on market close.

data.title: Eli Lilly launches weight-loss drug Mounjaro in India after drug regulator approval
data.description: Eli Lilly has launched its blockbuster diabetes and weight-loss drug Mounjaro in India following approval from country's drug regulator, it said on Thursday.

data.title: Bridgepoint Group Target Raised to 410p From 390p by Citi
Bridgepoint Group Raised to Buy From Neutral by Citi
Babcock International to Rejoin FTSE 100 Index After Seven-Year Absence
data.description:
By Ian Walker

U.K. defense company Babcock International Group will rejoin the FTSE 100 index on Monday after a more than seven years of absence, FTSE Russell said late Wednesday.

Babcock--which is a major contractor for the U.K. government--will replace investment firm Hargreaves Lansdown, which is being taken over by a private-equity consortium in a 5.44 billion pounds ($7.07 billion) deal.

Babcock, which was last in the top index between June 2012 and December 2017, has seen its share price rocket 44% over the past 12 months as geopolitical tensions fueled demand for defense platforms and governments commit to increased spending.

On Feb. 6 the company raised its guidance for fiscal 2025 after a strong performance over its fiscal year to date.

Babcock shares closed at 755.50 pence on Wednesday implying a market capitalization of 3.82 billion pounds.

Hargreaves agreed to be bought by the consortium--which is made up of CVC Advisers, Nordic Capital and a subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority--last August. Shareholders approved the deal on Oct. 14 and the company will delist on March 24.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: National Bank of Fujairah Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
National Bank of Fujairah (ADX:NBF) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Thursday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started Tuesday and was lifted after the United Arab Emirates-based lender published the results of its general assembly meeting.

data.title: RBC Downgrades Siemens as Valuation Shifts from Discount to Premium
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets lowered Siemens to sector perform from outperform, citing a more appropriate valuation premium after the stock's recent outperformance against the broader sector.

"We continue to like the business fundamentals, but with the stock close to our 245-euro price target, we downgrade to sector perform," RBC analysts wrote. "Siemens now trades at an 8% sector premium on EV/EBITA, having traded at an average ~8% sector discount since 2010."

RBC said the German technology group's ongoing review of its 75% stake in Siemens Healthineers has contributed to recent stock gains. While a decision is not expected until the company's December capital markets day, analysts noted that a divestment to just below 50% could release 15 billion euros, creating a balance sheet capacity of 34 billion euros.

Looking ahead, the research firm expects Siemens to generate 79.86 billion euros in revenue for fiscal 2025 and 85.01 billion euros for 2026. Net income is forecasted at 9.73 billion euros for 2025, declining to 8.51 billion euros for 2026.

data.title: NHPC Board Approves INR63 Billion Borrowing Plan for Fiscal 2025-26
data.description:
NHPC's board approved the proposed fundraising of up to 63 billion Indian rupees in fiscal 2025-2026, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The funds will be raised through secured or unsecured redeemable taxable non-convertible corporate bonds via private placement and/or term loans and external commercial borrowings in tranches, the filing said.

data.title: Boliden publishes Annual and Sustainability Report 2024
data.description:
Boliden

Boliden's Annual and Sustainability Report 2024 is now available at www.boliden.com. It highlights how Boliden creates value and covers topics such as strategy, operations, sustainability and financial performance. The report's overhead theme is A century of exploration and innovation.

Compared with previous annual reports, the Annual and Sustainability Report 2024 is adapted for digital reading with a landscape format and clickable navigation. A printed version, available in April, can only be ordered from Order Annual Report | Boliden.

The previously separately published Sustainability Index has been integrated into the sustainbility section of the Annual and Sustainability report 2024.

Boliden has also published the Annual and Sustainbility Report 2024 in Swedish in European Single Electronic Format (ESEF).

For further information, please contact:

Klas Nilsson

Director Group Communications

+46 70 453 65 88

Klas.Nilsson@boliden.com

This information is such that Boliden AB is obliged to make public pursuant to the Securities Markets Act. The information was submitted for publication at 07:45 CET on March 20, 2025.

Metals for generations to come

Boliden's vision is to be the most climate-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. We are Europe's producer of sustainable metals and, guided by our values care, courage and responsibility, we operate within exploration, mines, smelters and recycling. We are around 6,000 employees and have annual revenues of approximately SEK 90 billion. The share is listed in the Large Cap segment of NASDAQ Stockholm.

www.boliden.com

data.title: Alkhorayef Water & Power Technologies Bags SAR62 Million Leak Detection, Repair Contract
data.description:
Alkhorayef Water and Power Technologies (SASE:2081) secured a contract worth 61.8 million Saudi riyals from National Water Co., according to a Thursday release.

The water and wastewater management company will provide leak detection and repair services in Jazan, Saudi Arabia, for 30 months.

Hapag-Lloyd: Isn't Fully Foreseeable Yet How Tariffs Will Ultimately Impact Global Trade Relations
Power Assets' 2024 Attributable Profit Inches Up 2% as Revenue Falls; Shares Slide 3%
data.description:
Power Assets attributable profit inched up 2% to HK$6.12 billion in 2024 from HK$6 billion in 2023, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the energy-related investment holding company increased to HK$2.87 from HK$2.82 a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS per share at HK$2.95.

Revenue declined to HK$919 million from HK$1.29 billion a year earlier. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at HK$1.23 billion.

The company declared a final dividend of HK$2.04 per share, payable June 10 to shareholders on record as of May 27.

Shares fell 3% during Thursday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Fed's Reaction Function Seen as Complicated — Market Talk
data.description: The Federal Reserve's reaction function is really complicated, says Global X's Scott Helfstein. The Fed extended its pause on interest rates, as expected, raised its inflation forecasts and lowered growth projections. "They have to grapple with rising risk to both mandates, employment and inflation simultaneously," says the head of investment strategy. This isn't stagflation in the traditional sense, he says, adding that policy is having an outsize impact on consumer and business expectations. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Gulf General Cooperative Insurance to Submit Plan to Boost Solvency Margin
data.description:
Gulf General Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8260) said Thursday that it will file a plan to solve and boost its declining solvency margin to Saudi's Insurance Authority.

The insurer's move follows a notice from the regulator regarding its solvency margin being below its required threshold. Gulf General said it will have three working days to submit the plan.

data.title: Sany Heavy Chairman Proposes Up to 2-Billion-Yuan Buyback Program
data.description:
Sany Heavy Industry received a proposal from its chair, Xiang Wenbo, for the repurchase of between 1 billion yuan and 2 billion yuan of shares.

Under the proposal, the shares will be bought back via centralized bidding within 12 months from board approval, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the heavy machinery company were down 1% in recent trade.
